\documentclass{siddur}

% Custom page numbering to yield 120.a, 120.b, etc.
\renewcommand{\thepage}{120.\alph{page}}

% --- MACRO DEFINITIONS ---
\newcommand{\blockA}{%
  \begin{hebrew}
  קַבָּלַת פָּנִים \\
KABBALAT PANIM WELCOMING
  \end{hebrew}%
}

\newcommand{\blockB}{%
  \begin{hebrew}
  בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה, יְיָ \\
אֱלֹהֵינוּ, מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, \\
אֲשֶׁר קִדְּשָׁנוּ בְּמִצְוֹתָיו, \\
וְצִוָּנוּ לְהַדְלִיק \\
נֵר שֶׁל שַׁבָּת.
  \end{hebrew}%
}

\newcommand{\blockC}{%
  BARUCH atah, Adonai \\
Eloheinu, Melech haolam, \\
asher kid'shanu b'mitzvotav, \\
v'tzivanu l'hadlik \\
ner shel Shabbat.%
}

\newcommand{\blockD}{%
  BLESSED ARE YOU, Adonai our God, Sovereign of the universe, \\
who hallows us with mitzvot, \\
commanding us to kindle the light of Shabbat.%
}

\newcommand{\blockE}{%
  Shabbat Candle Blessing - The mitzvah of kindling Shabbat lights in the home is an early rabbinic \\
practice (M. Shabbat 2:1ff.) The Shabbat candle blessing is first recorded in the ninth-century \\
prayerbook, Seder Rav Amram. Lighting Shabbat candles as part of the synagogue service is an \\
innovation of Reform Judaism.%
}
